events.title: Birth of a Revolutionary Astronomer
events.summary: Nicolaus Copernicus, who proposed a heliocentric model of the universe, was born in Thorn, Royal Prussia.
events.details: His groundbreaking work challenged the long-held geocentric model, laying the foundation for the Scientific Revolution and fundamentally changing humanity's understanding of the cosmos.

events.title: Ohio Officially Joins the Union
events.summary: President Thomas Jefferson signed an act that officially recognized Ohio as the 17th U.S. state.
events.details: This act set in motion the mechanisms for federal governance in the new state, marking the successful and orderly expansion of the United States.

events.title: Thomas Edison Patents the Phonograph
events.summary: Thomas Edison received the patent for his phonograph, a revolutionary invention that could record and reproduce sound.
events.details: This remarkable device was the first to capture and play back sound, paving the way for the recording industry and transforming entertainment and communication.

events.title: Kellogg's Cereal Company is Founded
events.summary: Will Keith Kellogg founded the Battle Creek Toasted Corn Flake Company, which later became the iconic Kellogg's.
events.details: His innovative marketing of toasted corn flakes helped revolutionize the American breakfast, creating a beloved brand and a global food empire.

events.title: Old Trafford 'Theatre of Dreams' Opens
events.summary: The iconic home stadium of Manchester United Football Club, Old Trafford, hosted its very first match.
events.details: Known as the 'Theatre of Dreams,' it has since become one of the most famous and revered football grounds, bringing joy to millions of fans worldwide.

events.title: A Sweet Surprise in Every Box
events.summary: The first prizes were included in boxes of Cracker Jack, creating a delightful and beloved American tradition.
events.details: This simple marketing innovation brought joy to countless children and collectors for generations, making the 'prize inside' a cherished part of popular culture.

events.title: First Pan-African Congress Convenes
events.summary: Organized by W. E. B. Du Bois, the first Pan-African Congress met to advocate for African self-determination.
events.details: Delegates from 15 countries gathered to petition for an end to colonial rule, marking a landmark event in the global movement for liberation and equality.

events.title: Texas Formally Joins the United States
events.summary: The formal transfer of power occurred in Austin, officially making Texas the 28th U.S. state.
events.details: President Anson Jones declared the Republic of Texas was no more, peacefully completing the process of annexation and joining the state with the nation.

events.title: Cyprus Achieves Independence
events.summary: The United Kingdom, Greece, and Turkey signed agreements establishing Cyprus as an independent republic.
events.details: This agreement ended decades of British colonial rule and granted the people of Cyprus sovereignty over their nation, a crucial step toward self-governance.

events.title: A Spark for Second-Wave Feminism
events.summary: Betty Friedan's influential book, 'The Feminine Mystique,' was published, challenging traditional gender roles for women.
events.details: The book articulated the unhappiness of many housewives, inspiring a new wave of feminist activism and profoundly changing societal perceptions of women's potential.

events.title: A Formal Apology for Internment
events.summary: President Gerald Ford officially rescinded Executive Order 9066, which enabled Japanese American internment during WWII.
events.details: Ford's proclamation called the internment a 'national mistake,' a crucial and healing step in acknowledging the past injustice and promoting national reconciliation.

events.title: Iconic British Soap Opera Premieres
events.summary: The first episode of the popular British soap opera 'EastEnders' was broadcast on BBC One.
events.details: The show quickly became a cultural phenomenon in the United Kingdom, providing entertainment and sparking national conversations for decades to come.

events.title: Mir Space Station Begins Its Journey
events.summary: The Soviet Union launched the core module of the Mir space station, advancing long-term space habitation.
events.details: Mir was the first continuously inhabited long-term research station in orbit, pushing the boundaries of human spaceflight and fostering international scientific collaboration.

events.title: NASA Begins Mapping the Red Planet
events.summary: NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey spacecraft began its primary mission of mapping the surface of Mars.
events.details: The probe created detailed global maps and discovered vast amounts of subsurface water ice, providing crucial data for understanding Mars and planning future exploration.

events.title: Fidel Castro Announces His Resignation
events.summary: After nearly 50 years in power, Fidel Castro resigned as President of Cuba, enabling a political transition.
events.details: His resignation marked the end of an era and peacefully transferred power to his brother, Raúl Castro, creating new possibilities for the country's future.
